title: A Test Protocol and Its Test Log Are Not the Same Document, Even When They're the Same File
problem: pages/witness-committee-operations.html mixes two different kinds of content in one hand-authored HTML file: sections 00–04 and 06 are a forward-facing test protocol (opening question, per-document verification questions, the test sequence, a blank checklist template) that describes how to run a future Witness Committee test, while section 05 (Test Record Index) is a historical log of tests already run (TEST 01, TEST 01-B, TEST 02 pending). The file was built once, in one pass, with no structural line between 'the procedure for next time' and 'what happened last time' — because at the time it was written there was only ever going to be one test, then two. Claude raised a concern about the document's framing without first being clear about which part it meant, and Victor pushed back hard, correctly reading the initial framing as risking the same evidence-free pattern-matching mistake made earlier this session on wip/silent-god.json (see the convos_with_god / declarative-content lesson already in culture.md).
exploration: Claude's first pass conflated 'this document has a problem' with 'the historical test record is wrong' — which would have been the same mistake as before: judging content before being precise about what was actually being judged.

Pressed by Victor ('whats your counter argument? you wanna erase the record... why is your reasoning more important'), Claude distinguished: section 05 is testimony about the past (TEST 01 and TEST 01-B already happened; their logged results are evidence, not a draft) versus sections 00-04/06 which are a specification for a test that has NOT yet run again (TEST 02 is still 'Pending' in the same table) — a specification can be revised precisely because it hasn't produced a fact yet.

Victor confirmed the distinction was the real issue: the document has 'stale record data' in the sense that it was authored in one shot, before the site had any convention for separating a reusable procedure from an append-only log of runs of that procedure — not that anyone should touch TEST 01 / TEST 01-B's actual results.
decision: No edit was made to pages/witness-committee-operations.html in this session. The distinction — historical test results are immutable evidence; the protocol/template that produces future test results is a specification and may be revised — is the standing rule for this and any future test-record document. A structural split (protocol/template in one place, append-only run log in another) was proposed to Victor as a follow-up but requires his explicit go-ahead before any file is restructured.
lesson: When a single artifact contains both 'the rule for doing X' and 'a record of X having been done,' those two halves have different mutability rules even if they live in the same file: the record is closed the moment the event it describes is over (editing it after the fact is falsifying evidence, not correcting a draft), while the rule stays open to revision for as long as it hasn't yet produced the next recorded event. Before raising any concern about a document like this, identify which half is actually being questioned — 'this test's logged result seems off' and 'the next test's procedure could be run differently' are different claims requiring different justification, and confusing them (as happened here on the first pass) reads as exactly the same evidence-free judgment Victor already corrected once this session.
impact: No code or content change. Establishes the standing distinction for any future test/record document in this repo, and flags pages/witness-committee-operations.html as a candidate (not yet approved) for splitting into a revisable protocol/template part and a separate append-only test-log part, since it was authored before this repo had that convention.
